control.description-key: LeanToolKit home hub — three tabs. Calendar: every supplied meeting cadence projected onto a day/week grid, scoped to a person (crew-aware), area, department or site, with site-level protected time zones as coloured background bands; tapping an occurrence emits it with its boardId for navigation. Actions: the viewer's actions from every source, grouped and due-ordered, on the standard actions channel. Settings: calendar preferences plus the protected-time editor for site admins.

property.name: meetingsJSON
property.description-key: Every meeting to project: [{boardId, settingsJSON}] where settingsJSON is each board's MeetingScheduler settings blob (string or object). Cadence, topics, crews and the meeting identity (org, participants) all come from the blob.

property.name: protectedTimesJSON
property.description-key: Site-level protected time zones: [{label, color, days (names or indices), start HH:MM, end HH:MM}]. Rendered as coloured background bands behind the calendar — field leadership time, 1:1s, problem solving.

property.name: actionsInputJSON
property.description-key: The viewer's actions from the central actions table, standard action schema, any number of source cards. The Actions tab groups them by source and orders by due date.

property.name: actionSourcesJSON
property.description-key: Optional friendly labels for action grouping: [{instanceId, label}] mapping card instance ids to display names (e.g. board and card title). Unmapped ids fall back to the id.

property.name: peopleJSON
property.description-key: The roster for the person scope selector: [{whoId, who, crew?}].

property.name: viewerId
property.description-key: The signed-in person's whoId. Defaults the person scope to them, filters the Actions tab to their assignments, and drives the my-part-done toggle.

property.name: preferencesJSON
property.description-key: The viewer's stored preferences: {scopeKind, scopeValue, view, weekStart, dayStart, dayEnd}. Edits are emitted on Preferences Output for the app to persist per user.

property.name: canEditSite
property.description-key: When true, the Settings tab shows the protected-time editor; edits are emitted on Protected Times Output for the app to persist at site level.

property.name: selectedMeetingJSON
property.description-key: The tapped calendar occurrence: {boardId, title, iso, date, day, time, crew, shift, topic, selectedAt}. Navigate to the board and pass iso into its MeetingScheduler Select Instance input to land pre-selected. selectedAt changes on every tap so OnChange always fires.

property.name: actionsOutputJSON
property.description-key: The full current action set after an edit (my-part-done toggles), each action keeping its own instanceId — upsert to the central actions table by (instanceId, id).

property.name: preferencesOutputJSON
property.description-key: Emitted when the viewer changes a preference — persist per user and feed back into Preferences (JSON).

property.name: protectedTimesOutputJSON
property.description-key: Emitted when a site admin edits the protected time zones — persist at site level and feed back into Protected Times (JSON).

property.name: cardTitle
property.description-key: Optional title bar (e.g. My day). Leave empty for no chrome.

property.name: prompts
property.description-key: Optional empty-state text: plain text or a JSON array of strings.

property.name: backgroundColor
property.description-key: Hub background colour (any CSS colour or hex). Default white.

property.name: foregroundColor
property.description-key: Text / line colour. Default rich black.

property.name: accentColor
property.description-key: Active tab, today highlight, buttons. Default rich black.

property.name: titleBarColor
property.description-key: Background fill for just the title bar, distinct from the card background - use one colour across related cards to visually associate them on a board. Empty = no strip fill.

property.name: fontFamily
property.description-key: Font family (or comma-separated font stack).

property.name: readOnly
property.description-key: When true the hub is display-only: no action toggles, no settings edits (calendar navigation stays available).
